food_subgroup: Instant noodles with sauce and seasoning

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Korean buldak-style instant ramen with cheese flavouring; closest parent 'instant noodle food product' used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers dry instant noodles as the nearest defensible parent; no specific code for buldak cheese ramen exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to this specific branded Korean instant noodle product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one packet of Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun prepared as directed, yielding approximately 140 g of cooked noodles with sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Samyang Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Flavour Ramen — Nutrition Facts Panel (per 140 g serving / per 100 g calculated)
nutrient_source.organization: Samyang Foods Co., Ltd. — product label as reported in USDA Branded Food database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA Branded Food entries for Samyang Buldak Cheese Ramen (FDC ID cross-referenced with product label data). Per-100 g values calculated from the 140 g serving size declared on the product label. Values represent the prepared (cooked and drained with sauce) product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — instant noodles analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant noodles (wheat-based, boiled), which reports GI values in the range 47–58 (mean ~52). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 79.9) / 100 ≈ 41.5, where carbohydrate per 140 g serving = 57.1 × 1.4 = 79.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~1,214 mg per 100 g; ~1,700 mg per standard 140 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun due to its exceptionally high sodium content, which can significantly elevate blood pressure; a single serving provides approximately 74% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load with moderate GI (estimated 52) and high glycemic load per serving (~41.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the high glycemic load per serving (~41.5) can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and protein may help blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium and saturated fat content in an ultra-processed product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun, as its combination of very high sodium (~1,700 mg per serving) and saturated fat (~9 g per serving) in an ultra-processed format is associated with increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Buldak Hot Chicken Cheese Ramyun is a Korean instant noodle product produced by Samyang Foods as part of the popular Buldak (fire chicken) series. It consists of thin wheat-based noodles served with a thick, intensely spicy chicken-flavoured sauce enriched with a cheese-flavoured powder sachet, which adds a creamy, tangy counterpoint to the fiery heat. The dish is prepared by boiling the noodles, draining most of the water, and mixing in the liquid sauce and cheese powder. The result is a sticky, glossy, deeply savoury noodle dish with a moderate-to-high spice level and a distinctive cheesy aroma. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (~393 kcal per 100 g) and high in refined carbohydrates (~57 g per 100 g) and sodium (~1,214 mg per 100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index of 52 places it in the low-GI category, but the glycemic load per standard serving is high (~41.5), which is clinically relevant for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes. It contains wheat gluten, milk derivatives (cheese powder), and soy, and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with dairy or gluten allergies. The product is widely available across Singapore through delivery platforms and Korean grocery stores.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in Buldak cheese ramyun?
What is the calorie count of Buldak cheese noodles?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA Branded Food data for Samyang Buldak Cheese Ramen and cross-referenced with the product label; no exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for this specific product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-based instant noodles. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact primary regional source (HPB/ICMR-NIN) entry was found for this specific branded product.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the available USDA Branded Food label data for this product. 2. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the available USDA Branded Food label data for this product. 3. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 4. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 5. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 6. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 13. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 15. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 16. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 17. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 18. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 19. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. 20. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data for the prepared product. 21. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label declaration. 22. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label declaration. 23. The input description contained a humorous disclaimer about lactose intolerance; this has been disregarded as non-factual editorial content. The product contains dairy (cheese powder) and is NOT suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals. 24. food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Grains and staples' as the primary macronutrient and caloric contribution is from wheat noodles. 25. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local_names are null as this is a Korean branded product with no established Malay, Tamil, or Hindi naming convention; the mandatory multilingual requirement applies to Singaporean dishes, not Korean dishes. Korean naming is mandatory and has been fulfilled.
